description: Conduct thorough testing and QA for the new collaboration features to ensure they function as intended and meet user needs.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN collaboration features are developed WHEN QA is conducted THEN all functionalities are tested for reliability and performance.

story_title: Ensure Accessibility Compliance for Collaboration Features
description: Ensure that the new collaboration features comply with accessibility standards to provide an inclusive experience for all users.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN collaboration features are developed WHEN accessibility testing is conducted THEN all features meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

description: Implement simultaneous multi-user editing capabilities to allow multiple users to edit documents in real-time, preventing churn among enterprise customers.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a document is open for editing WHEN multiple users access the document THEN all users can edit simultaneously without locking.

description: Add presence indicators to show who is currently viewing or editing a document to enhance collaboration efficiency.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a document is open WHEN a user accesses it THEN presence indicators show active viewers and editors in real-time.

acceptance_criteria: GIVEN collaboration features are live WHEN users engage with them THEN usage metrics and abandonment rates are monitored in real-time.

description: Develop a formal conflict resolution strategy to prevent data loss during concurrent edits.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN two users are editing the same document WHEN they make conflicting changes THEN the system resolves conflicts without data loss.

story_title: Collect User Feedback on Collaboration Features

story_title: Implement A/B Testing for Collaboration Features
description: Conduct A/B testing on different versions of the real-time collaboration features to determine which configurations yield the highest engagement and satisfaction.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN different versions of collaboration features are deployed WHEN users engage with them THEN data is collected to analyze user preferences.

story_title: Competitor launched real-time co-editing — we need parity
description: Competitor X (Notion-adjacent) launched live co-editing on 2025-10-15 with a heavy marketing push directly targeting our enterprise segment. Sales has fielded 14 inbound inquiries in two weeks asking when CollabDocs will support simultaneous editing. Without parity we risk losing competitive positioning in the enterprise document space. This ticket tracks the initiative to deliver real-time co-editing at feature parity or better.

story_title: Current document locking causes user frustration
description: CollabDocs currently implements pessimistic locking: when a user opens a document for editing, it is locked to that user and others are placed in read-only mode. Support receives ~40 tickets per week from users who are blocked because a colleague has a document open. Common pattern: document left open in a background tab by a user who is not actively editing. Users are manually pinging colleagues on Slack to 'close the doc.' This is the most-cited UX complaint in our quarterly NPS survey.

Users have no visibility into who else is currently viewing or editing a document. This causes duplicate work (two people editing the same section simultaneously then manually merging) and communication overhead. Requested feature: real-time presence avatars in the document header showing active viewers and editors, with cursor position indicators per user. This is a prerequisite for real-time co-editing and has standalone value for read-only collaboration workflows.
